title: Maze Graphs, Connectivity, Cycles, Components, and Trees · Program Brief: Graph Mapping
summary: The solver treats walls as edges, generation visits display objects, disconnected cells are hidden, and tree claims rely on appearance. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the graph mapping increment in validated maze graph adapter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Map each cell to a vertex and each reciprocal open passage to an undirected edge without leaking render state.
Record action, source, focus owner, prior state, accepted transition, feedback, and changed binding evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-graph-topology-4
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-graph-topology-4.
# Case 1655: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: change display scale and retain focus and control access.
def worked_maze_graph_topology_4(vertices: int = 48, edges: int = 47, reachable: int = 48, components: int = 1, cycle_witnesses: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "gridworked4",
        "topology1655",
        "search4dekrow",
        "eventworked41655",
        "access1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if vertices < 1 or edges < 0:
        return False, vertices, "graph-count"
    if reachable != vertices or components != 1:
        return False, reachable, "connectivity"
    if cycle_witnesses > 0:
        return False, cycle_witnesses, "cycle-detected"
    if edges != vertices - 1:
        return False, edges, "tree-edge-count"
    assert edges == vertices - 1 and reachable == vertices
    return True, reachable, "validated-maze-tree"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Map each cell to a vertex and each reciprocal open passage to an undirected edge without leaking render state.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Graph Mapping model · Theory Graph Mapping, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Graph Mapping precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Graph Mapping model · Theory Graph Mapping, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 7325 during a classroom algorithm comparison. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate graph mapping, connectivity, components, cycles, and tree claims; the prior good state must survive failure; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Map each cell to a vertex and each reciprocal open passage to an undirected edge without leaking render state.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Graph Mapping model · Theory Graph Mapping, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Every neighboring cell pair is a graph edge even when a wall separates them.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: maze-graph-mapping-l77aji.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-graph-mapping-l77aji(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_graph_mapping_l77aji\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?vertices < 1 or edges < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?reachable != vertices or components != 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?cycle_witnesses > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?edges != vertices - 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
